title: Week 3 Day 2 (1/2) — Pipelines API
summary: Part 1 of 2 · High vs low HF API · training vs inference · Colab discipline.

sections.heading: Two levels of HF code
sections.remember: **Pipelines** = high-level, one-liner tasks. **Tokenizers + models** = low-level control (Days 3–5).
points: Pipelines wrap tokenizer + model + post-processing for common jobs.
Lower level: you choose model, tensors, `generate()` — more power, more steps.

sections.heading: How pipelines work
sections.remember: Create once with `pipeline(task=..., device="cuda")` — then **call** the object like a function on many inputs.
points: Optional `model="org/name"` from Hub; else HF picks a default.
sections.code:
from transformers import pipeline

analyzer = pipeline("sentiment-analysis", device="cuda")
result = analyzer("I am excited to learn LLMs!")

sections.heading: Training vs inference
sections.remember: **Training** fits billions of weights on huge data. **Inference** runs the fixed model on **new** input — all pipeline labs are inference.
points: Pipelines = inference API for business tasks (classify, summarize, translate).

sections.heading: Colab setup (day 2)
sections.remember: Pin versions when upstream breaks labs — e.g. `pip install -q --upgrade datasets==3.6.0`.

sections.heading: Colab troubleshooting
sections.remember: **CUDA required but not available** usually means Google moved you to **CPU** — not “install CUDA locally”.
points: Fix: Runtime → **Disconnect and delete runtime** → reconnect T4 → rerun from top (pip installs first).
HF **warnings / deprecation**: noisy but often safe — note them if something fails later.
Don't spiral into random `pip install` chains.

cheatSheet: CUDA error on Colab → reconnect GPU, rerun from top
